summary: The wireframe's 'Market Intelligence' section (market-intel-mi01: AU/US Demand Heatmap by Role, Remote Work Index, Hot Sectors, Salary Trends by role, Best Time to Apply, and an AI 'Where to Focus' recommendation + Explore matching roles CTA) is entirely absent from /dashboard. In its place ships the unrelated 'Real-Time Market Pulse' component (shared with the Analytics screen). This was invisible to the free-plan pass (masked by the paywall) — new information from this pass.
reproduction: Login as admin/admin123 (confirmed active_paid:true via GET /billing/entitlement)
Load /dashboard, scroll to the bottom section
Compare the rendered data-testid panels/headings against design/screens/dashboard.html's market-intel-mi01 block
Observe none of Demand Heatmap by Role / Remote Work Index / Hot Sectors / Salary Trends / Best Time to Apply / Where to Focus / Explore matching roles are present
Repeated in a fresh session with identical result
expected: The wireframed Demand-Heatmap-by-Role / Remote-Work-Index / Hot-Sectors / Salary-Trends / Best-Time-to-Apply / Where-to-Focus widget set including the 'Explore matching roles' CTA.
observed: A different, fully-functional, DB-driven 'Real-Time Market Pulse' widget set (Trend Indicators, Jobs-by-Source donut, Top Skills, Job Probability Score, Weekly Activity heatmap, Employer Hiring Activity, Recruiter Activity, Market-vs-You) with no equivalent AI-recommendation CTA anywhere on this screen.

summary: 'Top Skills in Demand' panel (inside Market Pulse) renders its header only with a blank body whenever GET /analytics/market-pulse returns topSkills:[] — no empty-state fallback copy, unlike every sibling widget on this screen. Root cause confirmed via DB: all 44 of this user's Job rows have an empty requirements array, so the server-side skill-lexicon match has nothing to match.
reproduction: Login as admin/admin123
Load /dashboard, scroll to Market Pulse -> Top Skills in Demand
Observe the header with no bars/rows/message beneath it
Confirm via GET /analytics/market-pulse that topSkills is []
Confirm via DB SELECT jsonb_array_length(to_jsonb(requirements)) ... GROUP BY len that all 44 Job rows for this user have len=0
Repeated in a fresh session with identical result
expected: Either populated skill bars or explicit 'No skills data yet' messaging, consistent with sibling widgets (Story Bank, CRM, Approvals, Agent Feed all show explicit empty-state copy).
observed: Blank void beneath the 'TOP SKILLS IN DEMAND' header.

summary: No plan-tier or quota/usage indicator is displayed anywhere on the dashboard hub (topbar user chip shows name + target role only; sidebar shows agent count only), despite the wireframe explicitly showing 'Pro plan' text under the user's name, and despite a real, populated quota system existing server-side (GET /billing/subscription) that is never surfaced on this screen.
reproduction: Login as admin/admin123 with confirmed active_paid:true
Inspect the topbar user chip and the sidebar bottom panel — both fully rendered
Search rendered text for any plan/tier/quota/usage string via regex /pro plan|plan:|quota|usage/i
No match found; repeated in a fresh session with identical result
expected: Some visible confirmation of plan tier (per wireframe) and/or usage, consistent with the brief's explicit ask to check whether the dashboard 'should show Pro now'.

summary: Two agent types that actively run in production (emailAgent, storyExtractor) are not registered in the Agent Activity feed's display-name/tile/badge maps (apps/web/src/components/dashboard/feed.ts), so they fall back to generic robot icon/copy ('emailAgent agent run completed') instead of dedicated readable copy like the other 5 agents get.
reproduction: Login as admin/admin123
Load /dashboard, read the Agent Activity feed
Locate entries for emailAgent (present in this account's real run history)
Observe generic 'emailAgent agent' / 'run completed' fallback copy
Repeated in a fresh session with identical result
expected: A friendly display name/icon/description consistent with scout/tailor/coverLetter/submission/supervisor.
observed: Generic 'emailAgent agent run completed' fallback copy and default robot tile.

summary: On a throttled connection, all 4 stat cards render as soon as the small/fast GET /analytics/funnel response resolves, without gating on the much larger GET /jobs?sort=fitScore response the AI Confidence card also depends on. This produces a misleading transient state — 'AI CONFIDENCE — no scored jobs yet' (implying zero data) for ~15-20 seconds under 50kbps/400ms throttling — self-correcting once the jobs payload finishes downloading.
reproduction: Login as admin/admin123
Apply CDP Network.emulateNetworkConditions (50kbps down/up, 400ms latency)
Reload /dashboard
Poll the AI Confidence card every 2s for 30s
Observe 'no scored jobs yet' from ~3.5s to ~17.6s elapsed, then self-corrects to '38% avg match quality' at ~19.6s elapsed
Reproduced twice: inline in session 2 and in a dedicated 30s polling follow-up run
expected: Either a shared loading gate across all 4 stat cards, or a card-level skeleton/spinner instead of the zero-state copy while its dependency is still in flight.
observed: Misleading 'no scored jobs yet' shown for 15-20s before self-correcting; no data loss, but the interim state reads as 'you have zero scored jobs' rather than 'still loading'.

summary: The Agent Activity feed's inline 'Approve' button for a coverLetter run's 'drafted a cover letter -- awaiting your approval' entry is driven by AgentRun.output.approval_status, a field cached at generation time that is never updated once the linked ApprovalRequest is resolved through another surface. Confirmed live: ApprovalRequest c3df4494103f796021d8aae69 has status:'approved' (resolvedAt 2026-07-17 16:34:47) yet its originating AgentRun's output.approval_status still reads 'pending', so the feed keeps showing an active Approve button for it indefinitely (still present 45+ minutes after resolution). Clicking it fails closed (409, no double-mutation) but surfaces a raw, unpolished technical string to the paying user: the literal HTTP method, endpoint path, record id, status code and a raw JSON blob concatenated into the toast.
reproduction: Login as admin/admin123
In the Agent Activity feed, find a coverLetter 'drafted a cover letter -- awaiting your approval' entry with a visible Approve button
(Verify first via DB/API read that the underlying ApprovalRequest is already resolved, to avoid mutating live shared data)
Click the Approve button
Read the toast: "Couldn't approve -- POST /approvals/c3df4494103f796021d8aae69/approve failed (409): {\"detail\":\"Approval already approved -- terminal state\"}"
Reproduced identically in two independent fresh browser sessions
expected: Either the button should not render once the underlying approval is resolved (fresh-check or hide-after-resolve), or at minimum the failure should surface a clean, non-technical message such as 'This request was already handled.'
observed: Button remains indefinitely; click 409s; raw REST method/path/id/JSON leaks directly into the user-facing toast.
